build_check.description: Headless launch exits 0 without fatal errors; this 0/1 gate multiplies the rest.

requirements.description: Each turn the player draws cards from a deck into a hand and plays them by clicking. Playing a card produces its effect (build, recruit, attack, or diplomacy) and removes it from hand. The hand refills next turn from the deck. 0 if there is no card draw, if cards cannot be played, or if playing has no effect. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Build cards add structures to a kingdom map that generate resources (farms produce food, markets produce gold, barracks produce troops). Resources accumulate and are spent by other cards. 0 if building has no map presence, if structures produce nothing, or if resources do not accumulate. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Attack cards spend troops to assault rival territories. Combat resolves based on troop count vs defense, with the outcome shown as territory gained or troops lost. 0 if attack cards do not exist, if combat has no resolution, or if territory never changes hands. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

Cards can be trashed (permanently removed from deck) to thin the deck and improve future draws. The player chooses which unplayed cards to trash each turn. Deck size is visible. 0 if deck thinning is impossible or if all cards are permanent. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

The full loop connects: title -> new campaign -> turns (draw/play/trash) -> victory/defeat -> replay or title. Victory requires controlling all territories; defeat occurs when the player loses all territories. 0 if any transition is missing or if win/loss is unreachable. Full credit needs authored sprites/illustrated assets; shape/default-widget versions cap at 0.5.

At least four card categories exist (build, recruit, attack, diplomacy) with at least two cards per category, each with different costs and effects. 0 if fewer than three categories exist or if cards within a category are identical. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The kingdom map shows at least four territories with different resource yields and strategic positions. Territories have visible ownership indicators. 0 if the map has fewer than three territories or if territories are identical. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

At least two AI rival kingdoms actively play cards, expand, and attack. Rivals have visible territory and grow over time. 0 if there is no AI opponent or if rivals never act. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

New cards are gained through building specific structures or winning battles, permanently adding to the deck. Gained cards are stronger than starting cards, creating power progression. 0 if the deck never changes or if new cards cannot be acquired. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

Diplomacy cards enable negotiation with rivals: truces that prevent attacks for turns, trade deals that exchange resources, or betrayals that break agreements for advantage. 0 if diplomacy cards do not exist or have no mechanical effect. Full credit needs distinct authored art; shape/color/text-only variants cap at 0.5.

The HUD shows current hand of cards, resource totals (gold, food, troops), deck size, and turn number in clear non-overlapping regions. 0 if hand, resources, or deck info is missing. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

The kingdom map clearly shows territory ownership with faction colors, structures built on territories, and rival positions. Cards in hand are readable with type, cost, and effect. 0 if territories are indistinguishable or if cards are unreadable. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Actions produce visible feedback: playing a card shows its effect on the map, attacks show combat resolution, building shows a structure appear, trashing shows the card removed, and territory capture shows ownership change. 0 if actions produce no observable change. Full credit needs themed UI with real assets; plain text/unstyled/raw-shape readability caps at 0.5.

Overall art coherence: cards, map, territories, UI panels, and effects belong to a consistent parchment-and-ink medieval visual language. 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Cards are real illustrated objects with themed frames, icons, and text that look like royal decrees or medieval documents. Different card types have distinct visual treatments. 0 if cards are plain text boxes or unstyled rectangles. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

The kingdom map uses real illustrated terrain with castles, farms, and territory borders that look like a medieval cartography style. 0 if the map is plain colored regions or a flat grid. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with medieval chrome: resource displays have icons, turn controls have wax-seal styling, and menus have parchment backgrounds. 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.

Animation and atmosphere: card plays, combat resolution, territory capture, structure building, and turn transitions use animation or tween effects. The map has ambient life (flags waving, smoke from chimneys). 0 if all events snap instantly. Programmatic shapes/solid fills/untextured geometry cap at 0.5.
